title: Dozens of states sue to block the sale of 23andMe personal genetic data
summary: States argue that biological samples, DNA data, health-related traits and medical records are too sensitive to be sold without each person's express, informed consent.

title: Drug deaths plummet among young Americans as fentanyl carnage eases
summary: U.S. drug deaths dropped by roughly 40% last year among people under the age of 35. It's a welcome pivot for families and communities devastated by fentanyl.

title: What happened when Lyndon Johnson federalized the National Guard
summary: President Lyndon B. Johnson federalized the National Guard in 1965, calling on troops to protect civil rights advocates who were marching from Selma, Ala., to Montgomery.

title: Trump mobilizes Marines and more National Guard troops for duty in LA
summary: U.S. Northern Command says the infantry battalion would be supporting the National Guard troops "who are protecting federal personnel and federal property in the greater Los Angeles area."

title: RFK Jr. boots all members of the CDC's vaccine advisory committee
summary: Health Secretary RFK Jr. has removed all 17 members of the CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices. He says replacing them with new members will help restore 'public trust' in vaccines.

title: Students from India and China Rethink Studying in the U.S.
summary: The United States hosts many students and scientific researchers from the world's two largest countries, India and China. The U.S. is seen as one of the best places to get and education and on the cutting edge of innovation. But a series of moves by the Trump administration, including greater scrutiny of those applying for student visa and, in the case of China, threats to revoke some visas, are making some rethink their plans.

title: Justin Baldoni's $400 million defamation suit against Blake Lively dismissed
summary: Baldoni's $400 million lawsuit was in response to Lively accusing him of sexual harassment on the set of It Ends With Us.

title: Sly Stone, visionary funk frontman of the Family Stone, has died at age 82
summary: The musical visionary led a multi-racial funk band that produced five Top 10 hits in the late 1960s and early '70s.

title: Pam Bondi's brother overwhelmingly defeated in heated race to lead the D.C. Bar
summary: The race became a microcosm for the clashes and pressures on the American legal system this year, in part because one of the two top candidates is the younger brother of Attorney General Pam Bondi.

title: Low turnout scuttles Italy referendum on citizenship
summary: The referendum, which sought to reduce the time it takes to become a naturalized citizen from 10 years to five, failed because fewer than 50% of eligible voters took part in two days of voting.
